design-lab-scatter-benchmark.v2._documentation: PR-A1.6c hard KPI gate config. Consumed by `scripts/ci/benchmark-1m-enforcer.mjs` against the artifact produced by `.github/workflows/benchmark-1m.yml`. Each case key matches the artifact `summary.medianRenderMsByCase` key (`fixture/tier/backend`). `medianRenderMsMax` is the absolute ceiling (the 1.5s public claim for WebGL); `baselineRegressionMaxPct` caps drift vs the latest `main` artifact at `gh api repos/.../actions/artifacts`; `requiredRunnerProfile` blocks GitHub-hosted SwiftShader runs from claiming the gate. Threshold edits MUST land via PR review.

uniform/million/webgl.description: Faz 21.11 PR-A1 leadership claim: 1M scatter WebGL warm render <1.5s, Chromium, animation disabled, fixture pre-generated, measured from setOption start mark to ECharts finished + 2 rAF, median of 5 runs.

uniform/million/canvas-lttb.description: Reference rail. Canvas + LTTB downsample (target 2K points). Held lower than the WebGL gate so the artifact comparison shows the size of the WebGL win.

correctness._documentation: PR-A2a anomaly-preserving LTTB recall config + PR-A2b CLI/workflow wiring (Codex thread `019e0faa` iter-1). Consumed by `pnpm bench:correctness` (which runs `tsx scripts/ci/benchmark-1m-enforcer.mjs --check-correctness-only`) AND by the pure `evaluateBenchmarkCorrectness(thresholds, runCase)` helper for unit tests. The benchmark-1m workflow runs this gate as a pre-flight before the artifact-driven render KPI step so a recall regression fails the PR check immediately. Threshold edits MUST land via PR review.

spike/million/anomaly-lttb.description: Anomaly-preserving LTTB MUST keep ≥95% of the 64 injected spikes when collapsing a 1M-point fixture down to 2000 points. `maxPrepMs` is advisory in the v2 introduction (CI runner variance); flip to fail-closed once the self-hosted GPU runner profile is stable.
